headlines.title: Former Trump adviser John Bolton to plead guilty to retaining national security information
headlines.description: John Bolton, who served as national security adviser during President Donald Trump’s first term and later became one of his fiercest critics, has agreed to plead guilty to one count of retaining national security information, two sources familiar with the matter said

headlines.title: Senate begins voting on Republican bill to fund ICE as GOP is split on Trump’s $1.8B fund
headlines.description: The Senate began voting on a bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol as Republicans split on Trump’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund.

headlines.title: CIA put senior officials on leave over officer arrested with gold bars
headlines.description: WASHINGTON — The Central Intelligence Agency put several senior officials on administrative leave over their handling of a high-ranking officer who worked on one of the most highly-sensitive programs in the U.S. government and had $40 million in gold bars stashed at his home, according to three people familiar with the decisions

headlines.title: Kennedy Center lawyers tell staff to remove references to Trump in signage
headlines.description: Attorneys for the iconic performing arts venue said the signage must revert to previous names like The John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts by June 12.

headlines.title: John Bolton expected to plead guilty for retaining national security information
headlines.description: Former Trump national security adviser John Bolton is expected to plead guilty to retaining national security information and could face up to 60 months in prison.

headlines.title: Blanche 'honored and humbled' that Trump plans to nominate him as attorney general
headlines.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said that he was "honored and humbled" that President Trump said he intended to nominate him to serve in the position permanently.

headlines.title: Trump to Tap Acting AG Todd Blanche to Lead DOJ Permanently
headlines.description: President Donald Trump is making changes at the Justice Department, nominating acting attorney general Todd Blanche as its permanent director. Blanche replaced Pam Bondi after her firing and since then has notably investigated several of Trump’s perceived enemies.

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: New York Democrats take first step toward drawing new congressional lines ahead of 2028
headlines.description: Years after handing redistricting power over to an independent commission, New York Democrats want it back as they join the national map-drawing battle with an eye on the 2028 election

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: New video offers a sneak peek into the Obama Presidential Center in Chicago
headlines.description: New video into the sprawling Obama Presidential Center in Chicago revealed a sneak peek of what the venue will look like.

headlines.title: Trump to nominate Todd Blanche to be attorney general
headlines.description: President Donald Trump will nominate his acting attorney general, Todd Blanche, to be the country’s top prosecutor, he said Wednesday evening.
